LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MATIASIC ROTH & JOHNSON LLP AND PAUL MATIASIC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION. OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND, THIRD AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION. SUSTAINED, WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. AS PLED IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, JUDICIAL ESTOPPEL DOES NOT BAR PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS AGAINST DEFENDANTS. FOR THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED AN ACTUAL PRESENT CONTROVERSY, AND THE COURT FINDS THAT DECLARATION IS NOT NECESSARY OR PROPER AT THE TIME UNDER ALL CIRCUMSTANCES. (CCP 1061.) FOR THE SECOND, THIRD AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS. TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANTS RAISE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING REPRESENTATION, THESE ARE NOT THE PROPER SUBJECT OF A DEMURRER. FOR THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANTS INTENDED TO DISRUPT THE PERFORMANCE OF THE CONTRACT. (CACI NO. 2201(3).) THE REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE UNOPPOSED AND GRANTED. DEFENDANTS ARE ADMONISHED FOR EXCEEDING THE PAGE LIMIT IN THEIR MOVING MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, WITHOUT LEAVE OF THE COURT. (CRC 3.1113(D).) PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. CROSS DEFENDANT VICTOR BRICE'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION ACCRUES WHEN THE COMPLAINING PARTY LEARNS, OR WAS PUT ON NOTICE THAT A REPRESENTATION WAS FALSE. (BRANDON V. GRAY (2003) 111 CAL.APP.4TH 29, 35.) THE CROSS COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT THE FALSITY OF THE REPRESENTATION WAS NOT LEARNED UNTIL THE FILING OF THE UNDERLYING COMPLAINT. (CROSS-COMPLAINT, PAR. 28.) THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS NOT UNCERTAIN. THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER THE TEXT OF THE LETTER SET FORTH IN CROSS-DEFENDANT'S REPLY BRIEF, AS A DEMURRER IS LIMITED TO THE ALLEGATIONS ON THE FACE OF THE PLEADINGS OR MATTERS JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE. (BLANK V. KIRWIN (1985) 39 CAL.3D 311, 318.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. CROSS DEFENDANT VICTOR BRICE'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT IS DENIED. CROSS-DEFENDANT VICTOR BRICE ("BRICE") HAS NOT MET HIS BURDEN OF MAKING A PRIMA FACIE SHOWING THAT THE CROSS-COMPLAINT ARISES OUT OF AN ACT IN FURTHERANCE OF HIS RIGHT TO PETITION OR FREE SPEECH. (CCP 425.16.) BRICE'S MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES DOES NOT ENGAGE IN ANY MEANINGFUL ANALYSIS OF HOW THE CROSS-COMPLAINT ARISES FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY. TO THE EXTENT THAT BRICE ARGUES THE CROSS-COMPLAINT ARISES OUT OF STATEMENTS MADE ABOUT HIS REPRESENTATION IN THE ROBINSON V. BRICE ACTION, THE DETERMINATION OF WHO WAS ACTING AS HIS COUNSEL DOES NOT "RELATE TO THE SUBSTANTIVE ISSUES IN THE LITIGATION." (NEVILLE V. CHUDACOFF (2008) 160 CAL.APP.4TH 1255, 1266.) TO THE EXTENT THAT BRICE ARGUES THE CROSS-COMPLAINT ARISES FROM HIS ANTICIPATION OF THE BRICE V. GUIDEONE ACTION OR THE BRICE V. MASTIASTIC, ROTH, & JOHNSON ACTION, CLAIMS THAT AN ATTORNEY BREACHED A FIDUCIARY DUTY TO A CLIENT, OR OTHERWISE VIOLATED ETHICAL RULES, ARE CONSIDERED NOT TO INVOLVE PROTECTED SPEECH OR PETITIONING ACTIVITY. (FREEMAN V SCHACK (2007) 154 CAL.APP.4TH 719, 732; SEE ALSO U.S. FIRE INS. CO. V. SHEPPARD, MULLIN, RICHTER, & HAMPTON (2009) 171 CAL.APP.4TH 1617.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. CROSS DEFENDANT VICTOR BRICE'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT IS DENIED. A MOTION TO STRIKE CAN BE USED TO REACH DEFECTS OR OBJECTIONS TO PLEADINGS THAT ARE NOT CHALLENGEABLE BY DEMURRER. (PIERSON V. SHARP MEMORIAL HOSPITAL, INC. (1989) 216 CAL.APP.3D 340, 342; SEE ALSO WEIL AND BROWN, CAL. PRACTICE GUIDE: CIV. PROC. BEFORE TRIAL (THE RUTTER GROUP 2013) PAR. 7:156.) A GENERAL DEMURRER LIES IF THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE BARS ALLEGATIONS ON THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT. (SEE E.G. SILBERG V. ANDERSON (199) 50 CAL.3D 205, 211; SEE ALSO WEIL AND BROWN, CAL. PRACTICE GUIDE: CIV. PROC. BEFORE TRIAL (THE RUTTER GROUP 2013) PAR. 7:60.10.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MATIASIC ROTH & JOHNSON LLP AND PAUL MATIASIC'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS GRANTED. BOTH SIDES HAVE MOVED TO ENFORCE THE SEPTEMBER 19, 2016 SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT, SO BOTH SIDES BELIEVE THAT THAT AGREEMENT IS ENFORCEABLE PER CCP 664.6 AND THE COURT AGREES. THE PARTIES' DISPUTE APPEARS TO BE LIMITED TO WHETHER THE PHRASE "CONFIDENTIALITY CLAUSE" AS USED IN THE SEPTEMBER 19, 2016 SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT INCLUDES TERMS REQUIRING THAT THE PARTIES AVOID DISPARAGING EACH OTHER. BECAUSE CONFIDENTIALITY OF A SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS A DISTINCT ISSUE FROM NON-DISPARAGEMENT, THE COURT CONSTRUES THE PHRASE "CONFIDENTIALITY CLAUSE" AS NOT PROVIDING FOR NON-DISPARAGEMENT TERMS. THE PLAIN LANGUAGE OF THE AGREEMENT CONTROLS OVER ANY EXTRINSIC EVIDENCE TO THE CONTRARY. COUNSEL FOR REVEREND BRICE IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 =302/HK

Plaintiff Victor S. Brices Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement, Or For Alternative Relief, To Declare No Meeting Of The Minds And Set The Matter Back On Calendar For Trial
Off Calendar Oct-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF VICTOR BRICE's Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement, Or For Alternative Relief, To Declare No Meeting Of The Minds And Set The Matter Back On Calendar For Trial -
Plaintiff Victor Brice's motion to enforce settlement agreement is granted. Both sides have moved to enforce the September 19, 2016 settlement agreement, so both sides believe that that agreement is enforceable per CCP 664.6 and the court agrees. The parties' dispute appears to be limited to whether the phrase "confidentiality clause" as used in the September 19, 2016 settlement agreement includes terms requiring that the parties avoid disparaging each other. Because confidentiality of a settlement agreement is a distinct issue from non-disparagement, the court construes the phrase "confidentiality clause" as not providing for non-disparagement terms. The plain language of the agreement controls over any extrinsic evidence to the contrary. Counsel for Reverend Brice is required to prepare a proposed order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS MATIASIC ROTH & JOHNSON LLP AND PAUL MATIASIC'S Motion To Compel - Defendants' motion to compel the deposition of Ms. Williams and for a further response to special interrogatory 48 is granted in part. At page 2 of their memorandum, defendants identify eight areas of inquiry they believe they need to depose Ms. Williams. The court finds that the first, second and seventh areas are either not relevant, do not rise to the level of extraordinary need to warrant defendants' deposing Ms. Williams about these areas and/or call for privileged information. The third, sixth and eighth areas can be adequately addressed by interrogatories directed to the plaintiff and thus do not warrant deposing Ms. Williams. MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-07-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jennifer A. Riso, Esq. of Ericksen Arbuthnot, Tel. 415.362.7126, for cross-complainant and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. CROSS DEFENDANT VICTOR BRICE's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is denied. Cross-Defendant Victor Brice ("Brice") has not met his burden of making a prima facie showing that the Cross-Complaint arises out of an act in furtherance of his right to petition or free speech. (CCP 425.16.) Brice's memorandum of points and authorities does not engage in any meaningful analysis of how the Cross-Complaint arises from protected activity. To the extent that Brice argues the Cross-Complaint arises out of statements made about his representation in the Robinson v. Brice action, the determination of who was acting as his counsel does not "relate to the substantive issues in the litigation." (Neville v. Chudacoff (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 1255, 1266.) To the extent that Brice argues the Cross-Complaint arises from his anticipation of the Brice v. GuideOne action or the Brice v. Mastiastic, Roth, & Johnson action, claims that an attorney breached a fiduciary duty to a client, or otherwise violated ethical rules, are considered not to involve protected speech or petitioning activity. (Freeman v Schack (2007) 154 Cal.App.4th 719, 732; see also U.S. Fire Ins. Co. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter, & Hampton (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1617.) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-07-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jennifer A. Riso, Esq. of Ericksen Arbuthnot, Tel. 415.362.7126, for cross-complainant and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. CROSS DEFENDANT VICTOR BRICE's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is denied. A motion to strike can be used to reach defects or objections to pleadings that are not challengeable by demurrer. (Pierson v. Sharp Memorial Hospital, Inc. (1989) 216 Cal.App.3d 340, 342; see also Weil and Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2013) par. 7:156.) A general demurrer lies if the litigation privilege bars allegations on the face of the complaint. (See e.g. Silberg v. Anderson (199) 50 Cal.3d 205, 211; see also Weil and Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2013) par. 7:60.10.) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-07-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jennifer A. Riso, Esq. of Ericksen Arbuthnot, Tel. 415.362.7126, for cross complainant and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. CROSS DEFENDANT VICTOR BRICE's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled. A cause of action for negligent misrepresentation accrues when the complaining party learns, or was put on notice that a representation was false. (Brandon v. Gray (2003) 111 Cal.App.4th 29, 35.) The Cross Complaint alleges that the falsity of the representation was not learned until the filing of the underlying complaint. (Cross-Complaint, par. 28.) The Cross-Complaint is not uncertain. The Court did not consider the text of the letter set forth in Cross-Defendant's reply brief, as a demurrer is limited to the allegations on the face of the pleadings or matters judicially noticeable. (Blank v. Kirwin (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re The Existence Of A Fiduciary Duty And Duty To Comply With Rule 3-310 Of The Rules Of Professional Conduct And Evidence
Off Calendar Apr-01-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Riso, Esq., 415.362.7126, for Defendants Paul Matiasic and Matiasic Roth & Johnson LLC; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling re:PLAINTIFF VICTOR BRICE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re The Existence Of A Fiduciary Duty And Duty To Comply With Rule 3-310 Of The Rules Of Professional Conduct And Evidence. Motion for summary adjudication as to three causes of action for declaratory relief, professional negligence, and breach of fiduciary duty is denied. Motion for summary adjudication on the issues of existence of a fiduciary duty and duty to comply with Rules of Professional Conduct 3-310 is denied. Each cause of action and issue of duty relies, as a threshold matter, on the existence of an attorney-client relationship. The formation of an implied-in-fact attorney-client relationship depends on the facts and circumstances of each case, and particularly on the parties' intent and conduct. (See Miller v. Metzinger (1979) 91 CA3d 31, 39; Hecht v. Sup. Ct. (Ferguson) (1987) 192 Cal.App.3d. 560, 565.) Plaintiff has not met its burden to show no triable issue of material fact as to whether the parties intended to enter such a relationship, where Plaintiff may have been represented by separate counsel by the time of Defendant's retainer by GuideOne Mutual Insurance Company. Additionally, Plaintiff has not shown that the work performed by defendant included legal advice, and was not of such a "preliminary and peripheral nature" as to avoid disqualification. (See Marriage of Zimmerman (1993) 16 CA4th 556, 5665-66.) As such, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether an attorney-client relationship was formed to give rise to a fiduciary duty and a duty to comply with Rule 3-310 of the Rules of Professional Conduct. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR5976
